steps.title: Use the new point's effect
steps.content: Data set A has best-fit line $y=-5.6+8.8x$, so its slope is $8.8$. The new point is $(9,52)$.

steps.title: Compare the new point to the old line
steps.content: Use the old model at $x=9$:$$y=-5.6+8.8(9)=73.6$$The actual new point has $y=52$, far below the old trend.

steps.title: Compare to the choices
steps.content: A far-right point below the old trend pulls the new best-fit slope down. The new slope must be less than $8.8$, and the only reasonable lower choice is $5.9$.
